## Entry 35
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:17:32.028Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:17:32.028Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_nMZbeE0WFyO547blcnEC4AV2",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 1624d9\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3135\nOutput:\n```\n\nAfter extracting `message_text` from ADK events:\n1. Call `conversation_service.upsert_session(db_session, session_id)`\n2. Call `conversation_service.append_turn(db_session, session_id, message, message_text, agents_invoked=[], latency_ms=<elapsed>)`\n3. `await db_session.commit()`\n\nThe chat route in `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` already gets `db_session` injected via `Depends(db_session)` — pass it through to `get_chat_response`.\n\n---\n\n## 5. Agent Architecture\n\n### 5.1 Agent hierarchy (exact ADK structure)\n\n```\nroot_agent  (LlmAgent, name=\"root_agent\")\n    sub_agents:\n        └── chat_agent  (LlmAgent, name=\"chat_agent\")\n                sub_agents:\n                    ├── flood_vulnerability_agent\n                    ├── heat_health_agent\n                    ├── infrastructure_agent\n                    └── logistics_agent\n```\n\nADK `sub_agents` are registered as a list on the parent agent. The parent can transfer control to them via tool call or explicit hand-off. The root_agent routes to chat_agent for all domain traffic.\n\n### 5.2 `metrosense_agent/agent.py` — root_agent\n\n```python\nfrom google.adk.agents import Agent\nfrom metrosense_agent.subagents.chat_agent import chat_agent\nfrom metrosense_agent.prompts import ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\nroot_agent = Agent(\n    name=\"root_agent\",\n    model=os.getenv(\"AGENT_MODEL\", \"gemini-2.5-flash\"),\n    description=\"MetroSense root orchestrator — routes all queries to the chat agent.\",\n    instruction=ROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION,\n    sub_agents=[chat_agent],\n)\n```\n\nroot_agent instruction:\n- Never answer directly. Always delegate to chat_agent.\n- Domain: Bengaluru climate, flood, AQI, power, traffic only.\n- Reject non-Bengaluru geography queries before delegation.\n- Reject harmful / non-domain queries with a fixed refusal string.\n\n### 5.3 `subagents/chat_agent.py` — chat_agent\n\nThe only agent that synthesises a natural language response to the user.\n\nTools on chat_agent: `resolve_location`, `list_document_indexes`\n\nSub-agents: `flood_vulnerability_agent`, `heat_health_agent`, `infrastructure_agent`, `logistics_agent`\n\nInstruction responsibilities:\n1. **Input guardrail** — if the query is not about Bengaluru climate/infrastructure, respond with: `\"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, air quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase.\"`\n2. **Location resolution** — always call `resolve_location` before forwarding to domain agents. Canonical `location_id` must be established.\n3. **Routing** — map intents to agents:\n   - Flood / lake / inundation / drainage → `flood_vulnerability_agent`\n   - AQI / air quality / health / heat / UHI → `heat_health_agent`\n   - Power / outage / BESCOM / tree fall / wind → `infrastructure_agent`\n   - Traffic / corridor / ORR / delay / logistics → `logistics_agent`\n   - Multi-domain → delegate to multiple agents in sequence, then synthesise\n4. **Scorecard trigger** — if query contains `risk`, `scorecard`, `vulnerability`, `assessment`, `how vulnerable is` → call ALL four domain agents and synthesise the Unified Risk Scorecard format.\n5. **Citation rule** — every factual claim in the response must reference either a DB record timestamp or a document section ID. No unsourced numerical claims.\n6. **Data freshness** — include the observed_at timestamp of the most recent data point used in the response.\n\n### 5.4 `subagents/flood_agent.py` — flood_vulnerability_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — from `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_historical(location_id, hours)` — from `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n- `get_lake_hydrology(lake_id, limit)` — from `tools/flood_tools.py`\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/flood_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — from `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — from `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nInstruction:\n- Primary signal: `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr` from weather + `fill_percentage` + `overflow_status` from lake hydrology\n- Secondary signal: `flood_incident` history for the location (frequency, severity, water_depth_cm)\n- Document grounding: read flood study index → identify relevant sections (ones with `contains_thresholds: true` for the ward) → fetch section → extract threshold values → compare against live data\n- Barricade recommendation: only if `rainfall_intensity_mm_hr > threshold_from_document` AND (`fill_percentage > 85` OR `overflow_status = true`)\n- Output in structured format (see §7)\n- Known monitored lake IDs (from golden data): Bellandur, Varthur, KR Puram, Hebbal — map these from location resolution\n\n### 5.5 `subagents/heat_agent.py` — heat_health_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_aqi_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `get_aqi_historical(location_id, days)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared weather tool (temperature, humidity)\n- `get_ward_profile(ward_id)` — from `tools/heat_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `aqi_value` + `aqi_category` + `dominant_pollutant` drive advisory specificity\n- `elderly_population_pct` + `children_population_pct` from ward_profile → sensitive population multiplier\n- `green_cover_pct` → UHI intensity factor\n- WHO thresholds applied per pollutant type (these are embedded in instruction, not from DB)\n- Requires specific advisory text per pollutant: PM2.5 → \"avoid outdoor exercise\", NO2 → \"keep windows closed\", etc.\n\n### 5.6 `subagents/infra_agent.py` — infrastructure_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_weather_historical(location_id, hours)` — shared (wind gust trend)\n- `get_power_outage_events(location_id, window_days)` — from `tools/infra_tools.py`\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- Primary signal: `wind_gust_kmh` (NOT `wind_speed_kmh`) from most recent weather observation\n- Only `outage_type != 'planned'` events are used for historical calibration\n- `fault_type = 'treefall'` events are the direct tree-fall risk signal\n- Document: BBMP tree canopy reference → tree density for the ward\n- Document: BESCOM infra reference → powerline proximity data for the ward\n- Risk tiers: `wind_gust_kmh < 40` = LOW, `40–60` = MODERATE, `60–80` = HIGH, `> 80` = CRITICAL (instruction-embedded, then calibrated by historical outage frequency)\n\n### 5.7 `subagents/logistics_agent.py` — logistics_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_traffic_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — flood tools (waterlogging signals)\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `congestion_index` + `delay_minutes` + `waterlogging_flag` are the primary traffic signals\n- `rainfall_mm_1h_at_time` on traffic observations provides the rain-at-observation context\n- `heavy_vehicle_share` matters for ORR and Hosur Road degradation rate\n- Document: flood study s4 (delay multipliers by corridor and rainfall band) → use for calibration\n- Key corridors: ORR, Sarjapur Road, Hosur Road, Bellary Road, Mysore Road, KR Puram\n\n---\n\n## 6. Tool Implementations\n\n### 6.1 `tools/backend_client.py`\n\nSingle `httpx.AsyncClient`-based module. Reads `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL` and `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` from environment.\n\n```python\nBACKEND_URL = os.getenv(\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\", \"http://localhost:8010\")\nINTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\")\n\nasync def _get(path: str, params: dict) -> list[dict] | dict | None:\n    \"\"\"Single internal HTTP GET with token auth. Raises on non-2xx.\"\"\"\n```\n\nAll tool functions call `_get()`. On `httpx.TimeoutException` or HTTP error → return empty list `[]` (tools must be fault-tolerant — a data source being unavailable should not crash the agent, just reduce answer quality).\n\nTimeout: `10.0` seconds per tool call.\n\n### 6.2 `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_weather_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent weather observations for a location.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_weather_historical(location_id: str, hours: int = 24) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get weather observations for the past N hours for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.3 `tools/shared/location_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def resolve_location(name: str) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resolve a free-text location name (e.g. 'Bellandur', 'HSR Layout Sector 2')\n    to canonical location_master entries.\n    Returns list of matches with location_id, canonical_name, ward_id, ward_name.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def list_locations() -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"List all known Bangalore locations in the system.\"\"\"\n```\n\n`resolve_location` hits `GET /internal/locations/resolve?name=<name>`.\n\n### 6.4 `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nDocument tools work **entirely from the filesystem** — no backend HTTP call. They read the mounted volume at `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n\n```python\nDOCUMENTS_PATH = Path(os.getenv(\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\", \"/documents\"))\n\nasync def list_document_indexes(\n    document_type: str | None = None,\n    ward_name: str | None = None,\n) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    List available document indexes.\n    If document_type is given, filter by index['document_type'].\n    If ward_name is given, filter to indexes whose wards_covered contains ward_name.\n    Returns list of index metadata (without full table_of_contents content — just section summaries).\n    Agents use this to decide WHICH document and section to fetch before calling fetch_document_section.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def fetch_document_section(\n    document_id: str,\n    section_id: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read and return the text of one section from a document.\n    document_id maps to the *_index.json file (e.g. 'bangalore_flood_study_2022').\n    section_id is the section_id field in the index's table_of_contents (e.g. 's2').\n    Returns the full markdown text of that section.\n    \"\"\"\n```\n\n`list_document_indexes` reads all `*_index.json` files from `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n`fetch_document_section` reads the `text_file` path from the matching index entry.\n\nThese functions are `async def` but use `asyncio.to_thread` for the filesystem reads (non-blocking).\n\n### 6.5 `tools/flood_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_lake_hydrology(lake_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent lake hydrology observations for a lake.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_flood_incidents(location_id: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get recent flood incidents for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.6 `tools/heat_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_aqi_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_aqi_historical(location_id: str, days: int = 7) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_ward_profile(ward_id: str) -> dict | None:\n```\n\n### 6.7 `tools/infra_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_power_outage_events(location_id: str, window_days: int = 30) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get unplanned power outage events within the past N days for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.8 `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_traffic_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Prompts (`prompts.py`)\n\nAll instruction strings live here. Never inline strings in agent files. Structure:\n\n```python\nROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nCHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nFLOOD_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nHEAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nINFRA_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nLOGISTICS_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\n```\n\nEach instruction includes:\n- Role and domain scope\n- Explicit list of tools available (agents reason better when told what they can call)\n- Output format for Level-1 domain payload (structured text with labelled sections)\n- Citation requirements\n- Data freshness: include `observed_at` of the most recent record used\n\n### Guardrail strings embedded in CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\n```\nOFF_DOMAIN_REFUSAL = \"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, \nair quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase your question.\"\n"
  }
}
```

calibrated by historical outage frequency)\n\n### 5.7 `subagents/logistics_agent.py` — logistics_agent\n\nTools:\n- `get_traffic_current(location_id, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name, limit)` — from `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n- `get_weather_current(location_id)` — shared\n- `get_flood_incidents(location_id, limit)` — flood tools (waterlogging signals)\n- `list_document_indexes(document_type, ward_name)` — document tools\n- `fetch_document_section(document_id, section_id)` — document tools\n\nInstruction:\n- `congestion_index` + `delay_minutes` + `waterlogging_flag` are the primary traffic signals\n- `rainfall_mm_1h_at_time` on traffic observations provides the rain-at-observation context\n- `heavy_vehicle_share` matters for ORR and Hosur Road degradation rate\n- Document: flood study s4 (delay multipliers by corridor and rainfall band) → use for calibration\n- Key corridors: ORR, Sarjapur Road, Hosur Road, Bellary Road, Mysore Road, KR Puram\n\n---\n\n## 6. Tool Implementations\n\n### 6.1 `tools/backend_client.py`\n\nSingle `httpx.AsyncClient`-based module. Reads `BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL` and `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` from environment.\n\n```python\nBACKEND_URL = os.getenv(\"BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL\", \"http://localhost:8010\")\nINTERNAL_TOKEN = os.getenv(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN\", \"\")\n\nasync def _get(path: str, params: dict) -> list[dict] | dict | None:\n    \"\"\"Single internal HTTP GET with token auth. Raises on non-2xx.\"\"\"\n```\n\nAll tool functions call `_get()`. On `httpx.TimeoutException` or HTTP error → return empty list `[]` (tools must be fault-tolerant — a data source being unavailable should not crash the agent, just reduce answer quality).\n\nTimeout: `10.0` seconds per tool call.\n\n### 6.2 `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_weather_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent weather observations for a location.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_weather_historical(location_id: str, hours: int = 24) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get weather observations for the past N hours for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.3 `tools/shared/location_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def resolve_location(name: str) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resolve a free-text location name (e.g. 'Bellandur', 'HSR Layout Sector 2')\n    to canonical location_master entries.\n    Returns list of matches with location_id, canonical_name, ward_id, ward_name.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def list_locations() -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"List all known Bangalore locations in the system.\"\"\"\n```\n\n`resolve_location` hits `GET /internal/locations/resolve?name=<name>`.\n\n### 6.4 `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nDocument tools work **entirely from the filesystem** — no backend HTTP call. They read the mounted volume at `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n\n```python\nDOCUMENTS_PATH = Path(os.getenv(\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\", \"/documents\"))\n\nasync def list_document_indexes(\n    document_type: str | None = None,\n    ward_name: str | None = None,\n) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    List available document indexes.\n    If document_type is given, filter by index['document_type'].\n    If ward_name is given, filter to indexes whose wards_covered contains ward_name.\n    Returns list of index metadata (without full table_of_contents content — just section summaries).\n    Agents use this to decide WHICH document and section to fetch before calling fetch_document_section.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def fetch_document_section(\n    document_id: str,\n    section_id: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read and return the text of one section from a document.\n    document_id maps to the *_index.json file (e.g. 'bangalore_flood_study_2022').\n    section_id is the section_id field in the index's table_of_contents (e.g. 's2').\n    Returns the full markdown text of that section.\n    \"\"\"\n```\n\n`list_document_indexes` reads all `*_index.json` files from `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n`fetch_document_section` reads the `text_file` path from the matching index entry.\n\nThese functions are `async def` but use `asyncio.to_thread` for the filesystem reads (non-blocking).\n\n### 6.5 `tools/flood_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_lake_hydrology(lake_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent lake hydrology observations for a lake.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_flood_incidents(location_id: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get recent flood incidents for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.6 `tools/heat_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_aqi_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_aqi_historical(location_id: str, days: int = 7) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_ward_profile(ward_id: str) -> dict | None:\n```\n\n### 6.7 `tools/infra_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_power_outage_events(location_id: str, window_days: int = 30) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get unplanned power outage events within the past N days for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.8 `tools/logistics_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_traffic_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 5) -> list[dict]:\nasync def get_traffic_corridor(corridor_name: str, limit: int = 20) -> list[dict]:\n```\n\n---\n\n## 7. Prompts (`prompts.py`)\n\nAll instruction strings live here. Never inline strings in agent files. Structure:\n\n```python\nROOT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nCHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nFLOOD_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nHEAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nINFRA_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\nLOGISTICS_AGENT_INSTRUCTION: str\n```\n\nEach instruction includes:\n- Role and domain scope\n- Explicit list of tools available (agents reason better when told what they can call)\n- Output format for Level-1 domain payload (structured text with labelled sections)\n- Citation requirements\n- Data freshness: include `observed_at` of the most recent record used\n\n### Guardrail strings embedded in CHAT_AGENT_INSTRUCTION\n\n```\nOFF_DOMAIN_REFUSAL = \"I can only answer questions about Bengaluru's climate, flood risk, \nair quality, power grid, and traffic. Please rephrase your question.\"\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 36
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:17:32.029Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:17:32.029Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_OB7DfNf0mgqMB4Gfs6nqQ8A9",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 22a6ab\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3625\nOutput:\n# UrbanClimate AI System — Architecture & Design Document\n### Bengaluru Climate Risk Intelligence Platform (Google ADK)\n### Version 4.0 — Normalized Schema, Agentic RAG, No Vector DB, 12 Tables Final\n\n---\n\n## Table of Contents\n\n1. System Overview\n2. Architecture Decisions & Rationale\n3. Agent Architecture — Final Design\n4. High-Level Query Flow\n5. Domain Agents — Detailed Design\n6. Structured JSON Output Design\n7. Output Modes — Text, Chart, Scorecard\n8. Data Architecture Overview\n9. PostgreSQL Schema — Final Normalized Tables\n10. Agentic RAG — Document Intelligence Layer\n11. Guardrails & Security\n12. Evals Strategy\n13. Three-Layer System Architecture\n14. Technology Stack\n15. Project Folder Structure\n16. Open Gaps — To Be Addressed\n\n---\n\n## 1. System Overview\n\nUrbanClimate AI is a **multi-agent orchestration system** built on Google ADK. It serves city planners, BBMP disaster teams, and logistics companies by fusing real-time sensor telemetry, PostgreSQL historical data, and unstructured documentation (IMD bulletins, flood post-mortems, drainage master plans) into actionable, explainable risk intelligence for Bangalore.\n\n**Core design principles locked in:**\n- Internal government use only for now — auth and public access handled later\n- Purely agentic conversation — no SSE, no streaming UI\n- Text-first responses — charts and scorecards are progressive, on-demand\n- Every answer must be grounded in cited data — no hallucinated risk scores\n- Data freshness indicator on every response so decision-makers know how current the data is\n- No vector database — documents are converted to structured JSON indexes and navigated agentically\n- Normalized Postgres schema — raw sensor/telemetry data only, no derived or computed fields in DB\n\n---\n\n## 2. Architecture Decisions & Rationale\n\n### Decision 1 — Agent Count: 1 Orchestrator + 5 Domain Agents, No Sub-Agents\n\n**What was considered:** Original design had 1 Orchestrator + 5 Domain Agents + 2-3 Sub-Agents per domain = 12-15 total agents.\n\n**Problem:** Too many agents managing context and state creates debugging nightmares. A failure anywhere in the chain is hard to trace. Testing compound queries requires mocking 4-5 agent boundaries simultaneously. The Orchestrator ends up owning complex glue logic which contradicts the thin orchestrator principle.\n\n**Decision:** Flatten to **1 Orchestrator + 5 Domain Agents**. Each domain agent directly owns its tools — both Postgres query tools and Agentic RAG document tools. Sub-agents are a refactoring step for later if a specific domain agent becomes too large, not a starting point.\n\n**Why this works for Bangalore scope:** This is a city-specific internal system in testing. Query volume and complexity do not justify the overhead of sub-agent management.\n\n---\n\n### Decision 2 — Structured JSON Output: A Discipline, Not an Agent\n\n**What was considered:** A dedicated JSON Output Agent that formats final responses.\n\n**Problem:** Adds a redundant LLM call, introduces a fragile parsing step, and creates an unnecessary agent boundary.\n\n**Decision:** JSON output is enforced as a **typed contract at every agent boundary** using Pydantic v2 strict mode. Every domain agent returns a Level-1 typed payload. The Orchestrator runs a pure Python merge function to produce the Level-2 Unified Risk Scorecard. No LLM call is involved in this merge step — it is deterministic.\n\n```\nDomain Agent Output  →  Pydantic Level-1 Payload  (per domain)\nOrchestrator Merge   →  Pydantic Level-2 Scorecard (unified)\n```\n\n---\n\n### Decision 3 — Output Modes: Text First, Progressive Enhancement\n\n**Three output modes exist:**\n\n**Mode 1 — Conversational Text (default)**\nEvery query gets a clean NL answer. This is always the primary response.\n\n**Mode 2 — Chart (on follow-up)**\nIf a chart would genuinely improve understanding such as trend queries or comparative queries, the system appends a follow-up prompt: \"Would you like to see this as a chart?\" User replies yes and the chart is generated. This keeps the user in control and avoids over-engineering the response layer.\n\n**Mode 3 — Risk Scorecard (triggered explicitly)**\nTwo trigger mechanisms:\n- **Button trigger** — dedicated UI button asks user for a ward/location and generates full scorecard for that zone\n- **NL trigger** — if the user's query contains words like \"risk\", \"scorecard\", \"vulnerability\", \"assessment\", \"how vulnerable is\" for a specific place, scorecard generation kicks in automatically\n\n**Important:** \"summary\" is intentionally excluded from NL triggers because it is too generic and can be used in non-scorecard contexts.\n\n---\n\n### Decision 4 — Zone Registry: Bangalore Major Wards Only\n\nThe system recognizes Bangalore's major BBMP wards as canonical zones. A `location_master` table with canonical names, aliases, and geometry is the grounding layer. This prevents the LLM from misinterpreting freeform location references.\n\nExamples: Bellandur, Sarjapur, Koramangala, Whitefield, Hebbal, Manyata Tech Park, Yelahanka, BTM Layout, Jayanagar, Peenya, KR Puram, Silk Board.\n\n---\n\n### Decision 5 — No SSE, Purely Agentic\n\nThe system is conversational and agentic. No server-sent events, no streaming token output. Responses are returned as complete payloads from agent execution.\n\n---\n\n### Decision 6 — Authentication: Deferred\n\nCurrently internal government use only. No authentication layer is implemented in v1. When the system is made public, role-based access will be added — specifically to protect the protected data tier covering power grid topology, sewage infrastructure, and building plans. This is a known open item.\n\n**Two data tiers defined for when auth is added:**\n- **Public tier** — weather, AQI, lake levels, traffic, flood history, ward-level risk scores\n- **Protected tier** — sewage and drainage infrastructure detail, power grid topology, specific building plans. BBMP and government roles only.\n\n**Security principle locked in:** Security cannot live only in the prompt. Protected tier tools must not be exposed to public role sessions regardless of prompt content. Tool-level access control, not prompt-level.\n\n---\n\n### Decision 7 — Data Freshness Indicator\n\nEvery agent response includes a `data_freshness` block telling the user when the underlying data was last updated. A BBMP disaster officer acting on 6-hour-old rainfall data needs to know that. This is non-negotiable for government internal use.\n\n```json\n\"data_freshness\": {\n  \"telemetry_lag_seconds\": 42,\n  \"last_imd_bulletin\": \"2024-07-15T06:00:00+05:30\",\n  \"pg_query_time_ms\": 310,\n  \"stale_sources\": []\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n### Decision 8 — Real-World Data Strategy\n\nThe Postgres schema is not a replica of any single real-world database. In reality BESCOM stores outage logs in a proprietary OMS, BBMP drainage data exists in scattered Excel files and physical maps, KSNDMC lake levels are published as PDFs and HTML tables, and IMD data comes as fixed-width text files.\n\nThe Postgres schema is a **designed consolidation layer** — real-world sources are cleaned, transformed, and loaded into this schema. For development the data is mocked to mirror realistic patterns. The developer will find real datasets from IMD, CPCB OpenAQ, KSNDMC, BESCOM annual reports, Uber Movement, and TomTom Move, then mirror them into the mock database.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Agent Architecture — Final Design\n\n### Agent Hierarchy\n\n```\nADK Runner (infrastructure — not designed, just configured)\n    │\n    ▼\nRoot Agent (Orchestrator — routing, state, guardrails, parallel dispatch)\n    │\n    ▼\nChat Agent (main conversational interface — only agent user talks to)\n    │\n    ├── Flood Vulnerability Agent      (sub-agent)\n    ├── Heat & Health Agent            (sub-agent)\n    ├── Infrastructure Stress Agent    (sub-agent)\n    └── Logistics Disruption Agent     (sub-agent)\n```\n\n### Key Decisions on This Structure\n\n**Runner:** The ADK Runner is not an agent you design. It is the ADK infrastructure that executes the Root Agent, manages the event loop, and handles tool call cycles. You configure it in one line — `Runner(agent=root_agent)`. It is not a component in the architecture diagram.\n\n**Root Agent:** Pure routing and state management. Never talks to the user directly. Owns parallel dispatch, global guardrails, and the Level-1 to Level-2 scorecard merge.\n\n**Chat Agent:** The only agent that has a conversation with the user. Owns session memory, NL synthesis, citation formatting, chart follow-up prompts, and scorecard triggers. It calls domain agents directly — no round-trip back through the Root Agent.\n\n**Domain Agents:** Pure domain reasoning. They are sub-agents registered under the Chat Agent. They own their own Postgres tools and Agentic RAG document tools directly. They never receive user input directly.\n\n**Important guardrail implication:** Because the Chat Agent is the actual entry point for user input, input filtering, injection detection, domain relevance checks, and citation grounding must all live at the Chat Agent level — not assumed to be handled upstream.\n\n### Sub-Agent Future-Proofing\n\nSub-agents are not used in v1. The signals to introduce them later:\n- Any domain agent's tool count exceeds 10-12 and LLM tool selection degrades\n- Any domain agent's agent.py file exceeds ~300-400 lines\n- Logic is being copy-pasted across two domain agents\n\nThe folder structure pre-partitions tool logic by concern inside each agent so splitting into sub-agents is a mechanical refactor, not a rewrite.\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                     USER INTERFACE LAYER                             │\n│     Natural Language Input — Planner / BBMP Team / Logistics Co.    │\n│     Internal Government Use Only (v1)                                │\n└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                            │ HTTP POST /chat\n                            ▼\n                    ┌───────────────┐\n                    │   BACKEND     │\n                    │   FastAPI     │\n                    └───────┬───────┘\n                            │ HTTP POST /agent/run (internal)\n                            ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    ADK RUNNER                                         │\n│                    Root Agent (Orchestrator)                          │\n│                                                                      │\n│  • Route intent, manage state bag                                    │\n│  • Parallel dispatch via asyncio.gather()                            │\n│  • Merge Level-1 payloads → Level-2 Scorecard                       │\n│  • Apply global guardrails                                           │\n└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                            │\n                            ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    CHAT AGENT                                         │\n│            Main conversational interface                             │\n│                                                                      │\n│  • Only agent the user interacts with                                │\n│  • Owns session memory and NL synthesis                              │\n│  • Input guardrails live here                                        │\n│  • Calls domain agents directly (Option A)                           │\n│  • Triggers chart follow-up and scorecard mode                       │\n└──┬──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────────────┘\n   │              │              │              │\n   ▼              ▼              ▼              ▼\nFLOOD          HEAT &        INFRA          LOGISTICS\nVULN           HEALTH        STRESS         DISRUPTION\nAGENT          AGENT         AGENT          AGENT\n(sub-agent)    (sub-agent)   (sub-agent)    (sub-agent)\n   │              │              │              │\n   └──────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────┘\n                               │\n                    Each owns Postgres + Document RAG tools\n                    Each returns Level-1 Pydantic Payload\n                               │\n                    Chat Agent synthesizes NL response\n                    Root Agent merges → Level-2 Scorecard\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. High-Level Query Flow\n\n```\nUser types in chat UI\n        │\n        │ POST /chat { session_id, message, user_role }\n        ▼\nBackend (FastAPI)\n  • Validates input\n  • Loads session history from Postgres\n  • Forwards to ADK server\n        │\n        │ POST /agent/run { session_id, message, history[], user_role }\n        ▼\nADK Runner → Root Agent\n  • Classifies intent\n  • Determines output mode\n        │\n        ▼\nChat Agent\n  • Applies input guardrails\n  • Resolves location to ward_id via location_master\n  • Fans out to domain agents directly via asyncio.gather()\n        │\n        ├──────────────────────────────────┐\n        ▼                                  ▼\nFlood Agent                      Logistics Agent\n  • Queries Postgres tools          • Queries Postgres tools\n"
  }
}
```

It is not a component in the architecture diagram.\n\n**Root Agent:** Pure routing and state management. Never talks to the user directly. Owns parallel dispatch, global guardrails, and the Level-1 to Level-2 scorecard merge.\n\n**Chat Agent:** The only agent that has a conversation with the user. Owns session memory, NL synthesis, citation formatting, chart follow-up prompts, and scorecard triggers. It calls domain agents directly — no round-trip back through the Root Agent.\n\n**Domain Agents:** Pure domain reasoning. They are sub-agents registered under the Chat Agent. They own their own Postgres tools and Agentic RAG document tools directly. They never receive user input directly.\n\n**Important guardrail implication:** Because the Chat Agent is the actual entry point for user input, input filtering, injection detection, domain relevance checks, and citation grounding must all live at the Chat Agent level — not assumed to be handled upstream.\n\n### Sub-Agent Future-Proofing\n\nSub-agents are not used in v1. The signals to introduce them later:\n- Any domain agent's tool count exceeds 10-12 and LLM tool selection degrades\n- Any domain agent's agent.py file exceeds ~300-400 lines\n- Logic is being copy-pasted across two domain agents\n\nThe folder structure pre-partitions tool logic by concern inside each agent so splitting into sub-agents is a mechanical refactor, not a rewrite.\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                     USER INTERFACE LAYER                             │\n│     Natural Language Input — Planner / BBMP Team / Logistics Co.    │\n│     Internal Government Use Only (v1)                                │\n└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                            │ HTTP POST /chat\n                            ▼\n                    ┌───────────────┐\n                    │   BACKEND     │\n                    │   FastAPI     │\n                    └───────┬───────┘\n                            │ HTTP POST /agent/run (internal)\n                            ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    ADK RUNNER                                         │\n│                    Root Agent (Orchestrator)                          │\n│                                                                      │\n│  • Route intent, manage state bag                                    │\n│  • Parallel dispatch via asyncio.gather()                            │\n│  • Merge Level-1 payloads → Level-2 Scorecard                       │\n│  • Apply global guardrails                                           │\n└───────────────────────────┬─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                            │\n                            ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│                    CHAT AGENT                                         │\n│            Main conversational interface                             │\n│                                                                      │\n│  • Only agent the user interacts with                                │\n│  • Owns session memory and NL synthesis                              │\n│  • Input guardrails live here                                        │\n│  • Calls domain agents directly (Option A)                           │\n│  • Triggers chart follow-up and scorecard mode                       │\n└──┬──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────┬──────────────────────┘\n   │              │              │              │\n   ▼              ▼              ▼              ▼\nFLOOD          HEAT &        INFRA          LOGISTICS\nVULN           HEALTH        STRESS         DISRUPTION\nAGENT          AGENT         AGENT          AGENT\n(sub-agent)    (sub-agent)   (sub-agent)    (sub-agent)\n   │              │              │              │\n   └──────────────┴──────────────┴──────────────┘\n                               │\n                    Each owns Postgres + Document RAG tools\n                    Each returns Level-1 Pydantic Payload\n                               │\n                    Chat Agent synthesizes NL response\n                    Root Agent merges → Level-2 Scorecard\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. High-Level Query Flow\n\n```\nUser types in chat UI\n        │\n        │ POST /chat { session_id, message, user_role }\n        ▼\nBackend (FastAPI)\n  • Validates input\n  • Loads session history from Postgres\n  • Forwards to ADK server\n        │\n        │ POST /agent/run { session_id, message, history[], user_role }\n        ▼\nADK Runner → Root Agent\n  • Classifies intent\n  • Determines output mode\n        │\n        ▼\nChat Agent\n  • Applies input guardrails\n  • Resolves location to ward_id via location_master\n  • Fans out to domain agents directly via asyncio.gather()\n        │\n        ├──────────────────────────────────┐\n        ▼                                  ▼\nFlood Agent                      Logistics Agent\n  • Queries Postgres tools          • Queries Postgres tools\n"
  }
}
```

APIRouter, Depends, status\nfrom pydantic import BaseModel, ConfigDict\nfrom starlette.responses import JSONResponse\n\nfrom app.api.deps import settings\nfrom app.core.config import Settings\nfrom app.services import agent_proxy\n\nrouter = APIRouter(prefix=\"/api\", tags=[\"chat\"])\n\n\nclass ArtifactPayload(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    type: str\n    title: str\n    source: str\n    description: str | None = None\n\n\nclass RiskMetric(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    probability: float | None = None\n    severity: str | None = None\n    congestion_score: float | None = None\n\n\nclass HealthAdvisory(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    aqi: int | None = None\n    aqi_category: str | None = None\n\n\nclass EmergencyReadiness(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    recommendation: str\n    actions: list[str] | None = None\n\n\nclass RiskCardPayload(BaseModel):\n    model_config = ConfigDict(extra=\"ignore\")\n\n    neighborhood: str\n    generated_at: str\n    overall_risk_score: float\n    flood_risk: RiskMetric | None = None\n    power_outage_risk: RiskMetric | None = None\n    traffic_delay_index: RiskMetric | None = None\n    health_advisory: HealthAdvisory | None = None\n    emergency_readiness: EmergencyReadiness | None = None\n\n\nclass ChatRequest(BaseModel):\n    session_id: str\n    message: str\n\n\nclass ChatResponse(BaseModel):\n    message: str\n    risk_card: RiskCardPayload | None = None\n    artifact: ArtifactPayload | None = None\n\n\nclass ErrorPayload(BaseModel):\n    code: str\n    message: str\n\n\n\n\ndef _error_response(code: str, message: str, status_code: int) -> JSONResponse:\n    return JSONResponse(\n        status_code=status_code,\n        content={\"error\": ErrorPayload(code=code, message=message).model_dump()},\n    )\n\n\n@router.post(\n    \"/chat\",\n    response_model=ChatResponse,\n    responses={\n        502: {\"description\": \"Agent unavailable\"},\n        504: {\"description\": \"Agent timeout\"},\n    },\n)\nasync def chat(\n    payload: ChatRequest,\n    app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings),\n) -> ChatResponse | JSONResponse:\n    try:\n        response_payload = await agent_proxy.get_chat_response(\n            settings=app_settings,\n            session_id=payload.session_id,\n            message=payload.message,\n        )\n    except httpx.TimeoutException:\n        return _error_response(\n            code=\"AGENT_TIMEOUT\",\n            message=\"The agent service did not respond in time.\",\n            status_code=status.HTTP_504_GATEWAY_TIMEOUT,\n        )\n    except (httpx.HTTPError, ValueError):\n        return _error_response(\n            code=\"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n            message=\"The agent service is currently unavailable.\",\n            status_code=status.HTTP_502_BAD_GATEWAY,\n        )\n\n    return ChatResponse.model_validate(response_payload)\n\n\n@router.delete(\"/chat/{session_id}\", status_code=status.HTTP_204_NO_CONTENT)\nasync def clear_chat_session(\n    session_id: str,  # noqa: ARG001 - placeholder until agents service supports reset\n    app_settings: Settings = Depends(settings),  # noqa: ARG001\n) -> None:\n    return None\n"
  }
}

httpx\n\nfrom app.core.config import Settings\n\nCHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 30.0\n\n\ndef _auth_headers(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n    token = settings.agent_internal_token.strip()\n    if not token:\n        raise ValueError(\"AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN must be set for agent requests\")\n    return {\"X-Internal-Token\": token}\n\n\ndef _extract_message_from_events(events: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:\n    for event in reversed(events):\n        content = event.get(\"content\")\n        if not isinstance(content, dict):\n            continue\n        role = content.get(\"role\")\n        if role not in (None, \"model\"):\n            continue\n        parts = content.get(\"parts\")\n        if not isinstance(parts, list):\n            continue\n        texts = [\n            part.get(\"text\")\n            for part in parts\n            if isinstance(part, dict) and isinstance(part.get(\"text\"), str)\n        ]\n        if texts:\n            return \"\\n\".join(texts)\n    raise ValueError(\"Agent response did not include model text\")\n\n\nasync def _ensure_session(\n    client: httpx.AsyncClient,\n    base_url: str,\n    app_name: str,\n    user_id: str,\n    session_id: str,\n    headers: dict[str, str],\n) -> None:\n    response = await client.post(\n        f\"{base_url}/apps/{app_name}/users/{user_id}/sessions/{session_id}\",\n        headers=headers,\n        json=None,\n    )\n    if response.status_code in {200, 201, 204, 409}:\n        return\n    response.raise_for_status()\n\n\nasync def get_chat_response(settings: Settings, session_id: str, message: str) -> dict[str, Any]:\n    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=CHAT_TIMEOUT_SECONDS) as client:\n        base_url = settings.agent_server_url.rstrip(\"/\")\n        app_name = settings.agent_app_name\n        user_id = \"metrosense\"\n        await _ensure_session(\n            client=client,\n            base_url=base_url,\n            app_name=app_name,\n            user_id=user_id,\n            session_id=session_id,\n            headers=headers,\n        )\n        response = await client.post(\n            f\"{base_url}/run\",\n            headers=headers,\n            json={\n                \"appName\": app_name,\n                \"userId\": user_id,\n                \"sessionId\": session_id,\n                \"newMessage\": {\"role\": \"user\", \"parts\": [{\"text\": message}]},\n                \"streaming\": False,\n            },\n        )\n        response.raise_for_status()\n        events = response.json()\n\n    if not isinstance(events, list):\n        raise ValueError(\"Agent response must be a list of events\")\n\n    message_text = _extract_message_from_events(events)\n\n    return {\n        \"message\": message_text,\n        \"risk_card\": None,\n        \"artifact\": None,\n    }\n\n\nasync def get_agent_health(settings: Settings) -> dict[str, str]:\n    headers = _auth_headers(settings)\n    async with httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=5.0) as client:\n        response = await client.get(\n            f\"{settings.agent_server_url.rstrip('/')}/health\",\n            headers=headers,\n        )\n        response.raise_for_status()\n\n    return {\"backend\": \"ok\", \"agent\": \"ok\", \"status\": \"online\"}\n"
  }
}

= tmp_path / \"Documents_Metrosense\"\n    dataset_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n    docs_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n\n    for name, body in CSV_FIXTURES.items():\n        (dataset_dir / name).write_text(body)\n\n    index_payload = {\n        \"document_id\": \"test-doc\",\n        \"wards_covered\": [\"Hebbal\", \"Bellandur\", \"Varthur\"],\n        \"table_of_contents\": [{\"wards_mentioned\": [\"Hebbal\", \"Nagavara\"]}],\n    }\n    (docs_dir / \"test_index.json\").write_text(json.dumps(index_payload))\n    return dataset_dir, docs_dir\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_loader_replace_and_idempotent(\n    db: AsyncSession, tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch\n) -> None:\n    dataset_dir, docs_dir = _write_golden_fixture(tmp_path)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DATASET_DIR\", dataset_dir)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DOCUMENTS_DIR\", docs_dir)\n\n    first = await loader.run_loader(replace=True)\n    second = await loader.run_loader(replace=True)\n\n    assert first == second\n\n    row_count = (\n        await db.execute(text(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM air_quality_observation\"))\n    ).scalar_one()\n    assert row_count == 1\n\n    location_count = (await db.execute(text(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM location_master\"))).scalar_one()\n    assert location_count >= 3\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_loader_rollback_on_midrun_failure(\n    db: AsyncSession,\n    tmp_path: Path,\n    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,\n) -> None:\n    dataset_dir, docs_dir = _write_golden_fixture(tmp_path)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DATASET_DIR\", dataset_dir)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DOCUMENTS_DIR\", docs_dir)\n\n    await db.execute(\n        text(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO location_master (\n                location_id, canonical_name, location_type, aliases,\n                ward_id, ward_name, zone_name, neighborhood_name,\n                pin_code, latitude, longitude, geometry, created_at, updated_at\n            ) VALUES (\n                'sentinel_loc', 'Sentinel Location', 'ward', '[]'::json,\n                NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,\n                NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NOW(), NOW()\n            )\n            \"\"\"\n        )\n    )\n    await db.commit()\n\n    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match=\"Simulated loader failure\"):\n        await loader.run_loader(replace=True, simulate_failure_after_truncate=True)\n\n    sentinel = (\n        await db.execute(\n            text(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM location_master WHERE location_id = 'sentinel_loc'\")\n        )\n    ).scalar_one()\n    assert sentinel == 1\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_location_validation_reports_missing_ids() -> None:\n    refs = loader.FactReferences(location_ids={\"A\", \"B\"}, ward_ids={\"ward_1\"}, lake_ids=set())\n    valid = [{\"location_id\": \"A\"}, {\"location_id\": \"ward_1\"}]\n\n    with pytest.raises(loader.LoaderValidationError, match=\"missing_location_count\"):\n        loader._validate_location_coverage(valid, refs)\n"
  }
}

\"feeder_id,feeder_name,substation_id,substation_name,outage_type,fault_type,cause_text,\"\n        \"affected_customers,critical_load_affected_flag,wind_speed_kmh_at_time,wind_gust_kmh_at_time,\"\n        \"rainfall_mm_1h_at_time,rainfall_mm_24h_at_time,source_name,data_quality_flag\\n\"\n        \"outage-1,evt_1,2023-01-01T14:21:00,2023-01-01T19:15:00,294,ward_001,ward_001,zone_north,\"\n        \"fdr_003,Hebbal Feeder A,sub_003,Hebbal SS,unplanned,treefall,Treefall on feeder,2269,True,\"\n        \"63.1,79.0,1.72,15.64,BESCOM,good\\n\"\n    ),\n    \"traffic_segment_observations.csv\": (\n        \"traffic_obs_id,observed_at,ingested_at,road_segment_id,road_name,corridor_name,location_id,\"\n        \"ward_id,road_class,lanes_count,speed_limit_kmh,avg_speed_kmh,free_flow_speed_kmh,\"\n        \"travel_time_minutes,free_flow_travel_time_minutes,delay_minutes,congestion_index,\"\n        \"heavy_vehicle_share,waterlogging_flag,incident_type,incident_severity,rainfall_mm_1h_at_time,\"\n        \"rainfall_mm_3h_at_time,source_name,data_quality_flag\\n\"\n        \"traffic-1,2023-01-01 00:00:00,2023-01-01 00:01:08,seg_orr,ORR,ORR,zone_north,,highway,6,\"\n        \"60,48.0,60,12.0,10.0,2.0,0.3,0.2,False,,,0.0,0.0,TomTom,good\\n\"\n    ),\n    \"lake_hydrology.csv\": (\n        \"lake_obs_id,observed_at,ingested_at,lake_id,source_name,water_level_meters,fill_percentage,\"\n        \"inflow_cusecs,outflow_cusecs,surplus_flow_cusecs,rainfall_last_1h_mm,rainfall_last_24h_mm,\"\n        \"overflow_status,gate_status,number_of_gates_open,alert_level,encroachment_risk_flag,\"\n        \"siltation_risk_flag,data_quality_flag\\n\"\n        \"lake-1,2023-01-01T00:00:00,2023-01-01T00:09:56,lake_001,KSNDMC,281.55,30.6,0.49,1.93,0.0,\"\n        \"1.14,4.12,False,closed,0,Normal,True,True,good\\n\"\n    ),\n}\n\n\ndef _write_golden_fixture(tmp_path: Path) -> tuple[Path, Path]:\n    dataset_dir = tmp_path / \"DataSet_MetroSense\"\n    docs_dir = tmp_path / \"Documents_Metrosense\"\n    dataset_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n    docs_dir.mkdir(parents=True)\n\n    for name, body in CSV_FIXTURES.items():\n        (dataset_dir / name).write_text(body)\n\n    index_payload = {\n        \"document_id\": \"test-doc\",\n        \"wards_covered\": [\"Hebbal\", \"Bellandur\", \"Varthur\"],\n        \"table_of_contents\": [{\"wards_mentioned\": [\"Hebbal\", \"Nagavara\"]}],\n    }\n    (docs_dir / \"test_index.json\").write_text(json.dumps(index_payload))\n    return dataset_dir, docs_dir\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_loader_replace_and_idempotent(\n    db: AsyncSession, tmp_path: Path, monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch\n) -> None:\n    dataset_dir, docs_dir = _write_golden_fixture(tmp_path)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DATASET_DIR\", dataset_dir)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DOCUMENTS_DIR\", docs_dir)\n\n    first = await loader.run_loader(replace=True)\n    second = await loader.run_loader(replace=True)\n\n    assert first == second\n\n    row_count = (\n        await db.execute(text(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM air_quality_observation\"))\n    ).scalar_one()\n    assert row_count == 1\n\n    location_count = (await db.execute(text(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM location_master\"))).scalar_one()\n    assert location_count >= 3\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_loader_rollback_on_midrun_failure(\n    db: AsyncSession,\n    tmp_path: Path,\n    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,\n) -> None:\n    dataset_dir, docs_dir = _write_golden_fixture(tmp_path)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DATASET_DIR\", dataset_dir)\n    monkeypatch.setattr(loader, \"DOCUMENTS_DIR\", docs_dir)\n\n    await db.execute(\n        text(\n            \"\"\"\n            INSERT INTO location_master (\n                location_id, canonical_name, location_type, aliases,\n                ward_id, ward_name, zone_name, neighborhood_name,\n                pin_code, latitude, longitude, geometry, created_at, updated_at\n            ) VALUES (\n                'sentinel_loc', 'Sentinel Location', 'ward', '[]'::json,\n                NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL,\n                NULL, NULL, NULL, NULL, NOW(), NOW()\n            )\n            \"\"\"\n        )\n    )\n    await db.commit()\n\n    with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match=\"Simulated loader failure\"):\n        await loader.run_loader(replace=True, simulate_failure_after_truncate=True)\n\n    sentinel = (\n        await db.execute(\n            text(\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM location_master WHERE location_id = 'sentinel_loc'\")\n        )\n    ).scalar_one()\n    assert sentinel == 1\n\n\n@pytest.mark.asyncio\nasync def test_location_validation_reports_missing_ids() -> None:\n    refs = loader.FactReferences(location_ids={\"A\", \"B\"}, ward_ids={\"ward_1\"}, lake_ids=set())\n    valid = [{\"location_id\": \"A\"}, {\"location_id\": \"ward_1\"}]\n\n    with pytest.raises(loader.LoaderValidationError, match=\"missing_location_count\"):\n        loader._validate_location_coverage(valid, refs)\n"
  }
}

## Entry 74
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:17:58.413Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:17:58.413Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_h7cAZhGw8oQYaU0F7K9kc1NP",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 17d2c2\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1260\nOutput:\n\n### Agents additions (`agents/.env`)\n\n```\nBACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://localhost:8010    # or http://server:8010 in Docker\nDOCUMENTS_PATH=/documents                     # mounted volume path\nAGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token       # same as backend\nAGENT_MODEL=gemini-2.5-flash\nGOOGLE_API_KEY=<your_key>\n```\n\n### Docker Compose changes (agents service)\n\n```yaml\nagents:\n  ...\n  environment:\n    - BACKEND_INTERNAL_URL=http://server:8010\n    - DOCUMENTS_PATH=/documents\n    - AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=${AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN}\n    - AGENT_MODEL=${AGENT_MODEL:-gemini-2.5-flash}\n  volumes:\n    - ./server/Documents_Metrosense:/documents:ro\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Backend Config Update\n\nIn `server/app/core/config.py`:\n\n```python\nagent_app_name: str = \"metrosense_agent\"   # was: \"metrosearch_agent\"\n```\n\n---\n\n## 11. `agents/pyproject.toml` Updates\n\nAdd to `dependencies`:\n```toml\n\"python-dotenv>=1.0\",\n\"pydantic>=2.7\",\n```\n\n`httpx` is already present. `google-adk` is already present.\n\n---\n\n## 12. Implementation Order\n\nExecute in this order. Each step is independently testable before moving to the next.\n\n### Phase 1 — Backend data layer (no agent changes yet)\n\n1. Add `ConversationHistory`, `AuditLog`, `DBSession` models to `server/app/db/models.py`\n2. Implement `server/app/services/data_service.py`\n3. Implement `server/app/api/routes/internal.py` with all 11 endpoints\n4. Register internal router in `server/app/api/router.py`\n5. **Test:** `curl -H \"X-Internal-Token: dev-internal-token\" http://localhost:8010/internal/weather/current?location_id=<id>` returns data\n\n### Phase 2 — Conversation persistence\n\n6. Implement `server/app/services/conversation_service.py`\n7. Update `server/app/services/agent_proxy.py` signature + DB write-through\n8. Update `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` to pass db_session to agent_proxy\n9. Update `server/app/core/config.py` (agent_app_name)\n10. **Test:** After a chat request, rows appear in `sessions` and `conversation_history` tables\n\n### Phase 3 — Agent tools\n\n11. Update `agents/pyproject.toml`\n12. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/backend_client.py`\n13. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n14. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/location_tools.py`\n15. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n16. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/flood_tools.py`\n17. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/heat_tools.py`\n18. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/infra_tools.py`\n19. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/tools/logistics_tools.py`\n20. **Test:** Import each tool module; call one function against live backend\n\n### Phase 4 — Agents\n\n21. Create `agents/metrosense_agent/prompts.py`\n22. Create domain agent files (flood, heat, infra, logistics)\n23. Create chat_agent\n24. Create root_agent (`agent.py` + `__init__.py`)\n25. **Test:** `uv run adk api_server` picks up `metrosense_agent`; smoke test a flood query\n\n### Phase 5 — Docker + integration\n\n26. Update `docker-compose.yml` (volume + env vars)\n27. Update `agents/.env.example`\n28. Run full smoke test through backend → proxy → ADK → domain agents → DB persistence\n\n---\n\n## 13. Testing Checkpoints\n\n| Checkpoint | What to verify |\n|------------|----------------|\n| Phase 1 done | All 11 internal endpoints return data for known location_ids from the golden dataset |\n| Phase 2 done | `sessions` + `conversation_history` rows written after each `/api/chat` call |\n| Phase 3 done | Each tool function returns non-empty list for a known location_id |\n| Phase 4 done | `adk web` can complete a flood query, a health query, and a scorecard query |\n| Phase 5 done | Full Docker stack end-to-end: frontend chat → backend → proxy → ADK → domain agents → DB |\n\n---\n\n## 14. What Is Out of Scope for This Phase\n\n- Streaming / SSE (confirmed not in scope)\n- AuditLog writes (schema exists, writes added in a later phase after agent invocation tracking is wired)\n- Chart follow-up prompts (agent instruction includes the hook, UI not yet wired)\n- Scorecard rendering in the frontend (agent returns structured text; UI RiskCard integration is separate)\n- Sub-agent splitting (current agent files stay flat; sub-agents introduced when any agent > 300 lines)\n- google_search tool removal (metrosearch_agent still exists and works; new agent replaces it for new sessions)\n\n---\n\n## 15. Known Risks\n\n| Risk | Mitigation |\n|------|------------|\n| ADK sub_agent transfer reliability | Test each routing path explicitly in Phase 4 before moving to Phase 5 |\n| `resolve_location` returning zero results | Chat agent instruction handles `LOCATION_NOT_FOUND` case and asks user to rephrase |\n| Documents volume not mounted in dev | `DOCUMENTS_PATH` defaults to a local path; document tools return empty list gracefully on `FileNotFoundError` |\n| DB write fails after ADK response | Best-effort persistence — log error, return response to user regardless |\n| `conversation_history` → `sessions` FK violation on first turn | `upsert_session` runs before `append_turn` in the flow |\n"
  }
}
```

    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_IITUO687UqELeiLt5n7UEcyg",
    "output": "Chunk ID: fac330\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 450\nOutput:\n### 6.2 `tools/shared/weather_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def get_weather_current(location_id: str, limit: int = 1) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get most recent weather observations for a location.\"\"\"\n\nasync def get_weather_historical(location_id: str, hours: int = 24) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"Get weather observations for the past N hours for a location.\"\"\"\n```\n\n### 6.3 `tools/shared/location_tools.py`\n\n```python\nasync def resolve_location(name: str) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resolve a free-text location name (e.g. 'Bellandur', 'HSR Layout Sector 2')\n    to canonical location_master entries.\n    Returns list of matches with location_id, canonical_name, ward_id, ward_name.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def list_locations() -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"List all known Bangalore locations in the system.\"\"\"\n```\n\n`resolve_location` hits `GET /internal/locations/resolve?name=<name>`.\n\n### 6.4 `tools/shared/document_tools.py`\n\nDocument tools work **entirely from the filesystem** — no backend HTTP call. They read the mounted volume at `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n\n```python\nDOCUMENTS_PATH = Path(os.getenv(\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\", \"/documents\"))\n\nasync def list_document_indexes(\n    document_type: str | None = None,\n    ward_name: str | None = None,\n) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    List available document indexes.\n    If document_type is given, filter by index['document_type'].\n    If ward_name is given, filter to indexes whose wards_covered contains ward_name.\n    Returns list of index metadata (without full table_of_contents content — just section summaries).\n    Agents use this to decide WHICH document and section to fetch before calling fetch_document_section.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def fetch_document_section(\n    document_id: str,\n    section_id: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read and return the text of one section from a document.\n"
  }
}
```

They read the mounted volume at `DOCUMENTS_PATH`.\n\n```python\nDOCUMENTS_PATH = Path(os.getenv(\"DOCUMENTS_PATH\", \"/documents\"))\n\nasync def list_document_indexes(\n    document_type: str | None = None,\n    ward_name: str | None = None,\n) -> list[dict]:\n    \"\"\"\n    List available document indexes.\n    If document_type is given, filter by index['document_type'].\n    If ward_name is given, filter to indexes whose wards_covered contains ward_name.\n    Returns list of index metadata (without full table_of_contents content — just section summaries).\n    Agents use this to decide WHICH document and section to fetch before calling fetch_document_section.\n    \"\"\"\n\nasync def fetch_document_section(\n    document_id: str,\n    section_id: str,\n) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Read and return the text of one section from a document.\n"

    "call_id": "call_4D6ypdrdhY3H8nU2TlPUnAX2",
    "output": "Chunk ID: dd16a7\nWall time: 0.0019 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 2010\nOutput:\n# MetroSense\n\nMetroSense is a chat-first climate and infrastructure intelligence system for Bengaluru, built with FastAPI, React, and PostgreSQL. This repo includes the backend, frontend, and internal agents service plus the tooling and quality gates needed to evolve the product.\n\n## Tech Stack\n\n| Layer | Technology |\n|-------|-----------|\n| Backend | FastAPI, SQLAlchemy 2.0 (async), Alembic, PostgreSQL 16 |\n| Frontend | React 18, TypeScript, Vite 5, Material-UI v6 |\n| Package Managers | uv (Python), pnpm (Node) |\n| Quality | Ruff, MyPy (strict), import-linter, ESLint, TypeScript strict |\n| Containers | Docker, Docker Compose |\n| CI | GitHub Actions |\n\n## Project Structure\n\n```\n.\n├── .github/workflows/ci.yml    # CI pipeline\n├── docker-compose.yml           # PostgreSQL + API + Client\n├── Dockerfile                   # Multi-stage production build\n├── server/                      # FastAPI backend\n│   ├── app/\n│   │   ├── api/routes/          # HTTP endpoints\n│   │   ├── core/                # Config + logging\n│   │   ├── db/                  # SQLAlchemy models + session\n│   │   ├── middleware/          # Error handler + request logging\n│   │   ├── services/            # Business logic layer\n│   │   └── main.py              # App factory\n│   ├── alembic/                 # Database migrations\n│   ├── tests/                   # Unit + integration tests\n│   └── pyproject.toml           # Dependencies + tool config\n├── agents/                      # ADK agent service (internal)\n│   ├── metrosearch_agent/        # Simple Google Search-enabled agent\n│   ├── scripts/                 # Smoke tests\n│   └── pyproject.toml           # Dependencies + tool config\n└── client/                      # React frontend\n    ├── src/\n    │   ├── App.tsx              # Router + pages\n    │   ├── main.tsx             # Entry point + providers\n    │   └── lib/api.ts           # Axios instance\n    ├── package.json             # Dependencies + scripts\n    └── Dockerfile               # Node build → Nginx\n```\n\n## Module Boundaries\n\n- `app.api` depends on `app.services`, `app.core` only\n- `app.services` depends on `app.db`, `app.core` only (must NOT import from `app.api`)\n- `app.db` depends on `app.core` only (must NOT import from `app.services` or `app.api`)\n- Enforced by `import-linter` in CI\n\n## Quick Start\n\nThe agents service is protected by a shared secret header in dev. The backend talks to an agents proxy on port 8020, and that proxy forwards to the ADK server on port 8021. Set the same `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` in both services so the proxy can validate requests.\n\n```bash\n# Start PostgreSQL\ndocker-compose up -d db\n\n# Backend\ncd server\nuv sync --all-extras\nuv run alembic upgrade head\nexport AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token\nuv run uvicorn app.main:app --reload --port 8010\n\n# Agents (separate terminals)\ncd agents\nuv sync\nexport GOOGLE_API_KEY=your_key\nexport AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token\nuv run adk api_server --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8021 .\n\n# Agents proxy (separate terminal)\ncd agents\nexport AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN=dev-internal-token\nuv run uvicorn app.proxy:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8020\n\n# Optional: ADK Web UI (separate terminal)\ncd agents\nuv run adk web --port 8001\n\n# Frontend (separate terminal)\ncd client\npnpm install\npnpm run dev\n```\n\nApp runs at http://localhost:5173 with API proxied to :8010.\n\n## Quality Gates\n\nBackend (from `server/`):\n```bash\nuv run ruff format --check .    # Formatting\nuv run ruff check .             # Linting\nuv run mypy .                   # Type checking (strict)\nuv run lint-imports             # Module boundaries\nuv run pytest tests/unit -q     # Unit tests\nuv run pytest tests/integration -q  # Integration tests\n```\n\nFrontend (from `client/`):\n```bash\npnpm run lint   # ESLint\npnpm run build  # TypeScript + Vite build\n```\n\nFrontend E2E (from `client/`):\n```bash\npnpm exec playwright install\npnpm run test:e2e\n```\n\n## MetroSense Golden Dataset\n\nFor the implemented Postgres schema + CSV loader for MetroSense golden data, see:\n\n- `docs/METROSENSE_DATASET_SCHEMA_AND_LOADER.md`\n\n## API Endpoints\n\n| Method | Path | Auth | Description |\n|--------|------|------|-------------|\n| GET | `/health` | No | Health check |\n| POST | `/api/auth/signup` | No | Create account + set JWT cookie |\n| POST | `/api/auth/login` | No | Login + set JWT cookie |\n| POST | `/api/auth/logout` | Yes | Clear JWT cookie |\n| GET | `/api/auth/me` | Yes | Current user |\n| POST | `/api/chat` | Yes | Chat request (currently returns text-only; risk cards and artifacts are planned) |\n\n**Note:** The `/api/chat` endpoint currently routes requests to the agent service and retumns streamed or buffered text responses. Risk card and artifact generation (structured responses) are planned and will be implemented once the agent layer is equipped with domain tools and data access.\n\n## Environment Variables\n\n### Backend (server/)\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------||\n| `ENV` | `development` | development / test / production |\n| `DATABASE_URL` | `postgresql+asyncpg://postgres:postgres@localhost:5433/app_scaffold` | Database connection |\n| `JWT_SECRET` | `app-scaffold-dev-secret` | JWT signing secret |\n| `JWT_ALGORITHM` | `HS256` | JWT algorithm |\n| `JWT_EXPIRES_MINUTES` | `60` | JWT expiration in minutes |\n| `AUTH_COOKIE_NAME` | `metrosense_token` | Auth cookie name |\n| `CORS_ORIGINS` | `http://localhost:5173,http://127.0.0.1:5173` | Allowed origins for cookies |\n| `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` | *(required)* | Shared secret for backend-to-agent proxy authentication (e.g., `dev-internal-token` in dev) |\n| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://localhost:8020` | Internal URL for agents proxy |\n\n### Agents (agents/)\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------||\n| `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | *(required)* | Google Generative AI API key for Gemini model |\n| `AGENT_INTERNAL_TOKEN` | *(required)* | Shared secret for proxy authentication (must match backend) |\n| `AGENT_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` | Gemini model to use |\n| `ADK_BASE_URL` | `http://localhost:8021` | (optional) ADK API server URL |\n\n## Current Implementation Status\n\n### What Works (✅)\n- **Auth:** Signup, login, logout, JWT cookies, password hashing\n- **Chat Endpoint:** Routes messages to agent service; returns text responses\n- **Database:** Schema defined for weather, AQI, flooding, outages, traffic, and location data\n- **Backend-to-Agent Proxy:** Validates X-Internal-Token, forwards sessions to ADK\n- **Frontend UI:** Chat interface, login/signup, message display, component stubs for RiskCard and Artifacts\n- **Health Checks:** Backend and composite backend+agent status endpoints\n\n### What's Missing (📋)\n- **Data Loading:** CSV files and documents exist but are not yet seeded into PostgreSQL\n- **Agent Tools:** Only google_search available; custom flood-risk, AQI, outage, and traffic tools not yet implemented\n- **Structured Responses:** Risk cards and artifacts are rendered in UI templates but not yet generated by agent\n- **Streaming:** Responses are single POST/response; SSE or WebSocket streaming not yet implemented\n- **Message History:** Conversations stored in-memory only; database persistence not yet implemented\n- **E2E Tests:** Playwright configuration exists but test suites not written\n\n### Next Steps\nSee **Next Steps (Recommended Order)** in [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#next-steps-recommended-order) for the development roadmap.\n\n## Docker\n\n```bash\n# Development (3 services)\ndocker-compose up\n\n# Production (single image)\ndocker build -t metrosense .\n```\n\n## Coding Style\n\n- Python: 4-space indent, type hints, `snake_case` functions, `PascalCase` classes\n- TypeScript: strict mode, `PascalCase` components, `camelCase` utilities\n- Backend layering: Routes → Services → DB (enforced)\n- Conventional Commits: `feat:`, `fix:`, `refactor:`\n"
  }
}
```

    "output": "Chunk ID: ae203e\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 320\nOutput:\n[project]\nname = \"app-scaffold\"\nversion = \"0.1.0\"\ndescription = \"Full-stack scaffold — FastAPI + React + PostgreSQL + Google ADK\"\nrequires-python = \">=3.12\"\ndependencies = [\n  \"fastapi>=0.111\",\n  \"uvicorn[standard]>=0.30\",\n  \"httpx>=0.27\",\n  \"pydantic[email]>=2.7\",\n  \"pydantic-settings>=2.3\",\n  \"bcrypt<4\",\n  \"passlib[bcrypt]>=1.7\",\n  \"pyjwt>=2.9\",\n  \"sqlalchemy>=2.0\",\n  \"asyncpg>=0.30\",\n  \"alembic>=1.14\",\n  \"loguru>=0.7\",\n]\n\n[project.optional-dependencies]\ndev = [\n  \"pytest>=8.0\",\n  \"pytest-asyncio>=0.23\",\n  \"ruff>=0.5\",\n  \"mypy>=1.10\",\n  \"import-linter>=2.0\",\n]\n\n[tool.uv]\nmanaged = true\n\n[tool.ruff]\nline-length = 100\n\n[tool.ruff.lint]\nselect = [\"E\", \"F\", \"I\", \"B\", \"UP\"]\nignore = [\"B008\"]\n\n[tool.pytest.ini_options]\nasyncio_mode = \"auto\"\ntestpaths = [\"tests/unit\", \"tests/integration\"]\n\n[tool.mypy]\npython_version = \"3.12\"\nstrict = true\nplugins = [\"pydantic.mypy\"]\nexclude = [\"alembic/\"]\n\n[tool.importlinter]\nroot_packages = [\"app\"]\n\n[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]\nname = \"Services do not import from API\"\ntype = \"forbidden\"\nsource_modules = [\"app.services\"]\nforbidden_modules = [\"app.api\"]\n\n[[tool.importlinter.contracts]]\nname = \"DB does not import from services or API\"\ntype = \"forbidden\"\nsource_modules = [\"app.db\"]\nforbidden_modules = [\"app.services\", \"app.api\"]\n"
  }
}

## Entry 100
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-11T09:18:17.276Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-11T09:18:17.276Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_bghVF1HG3RdRO1EO5V5MM6O7",
    "output": "Chunk ID: da1660\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 691\nOutput:\n# MetroSense Dataset Schema and Loader\n\nThis documents the backend changes implemented for loading MetroSense golden data into PostgreSQL.\n\n## What was implemented\n\n- Added MetroSense database schema (12 architecture tables) while keeping existing auth `users` table.\n- Added a new Alembic migration to create the schema.\n- Added an idempotent loader that reads directly from CSV files in:\n  - `server/DataSet_MetroSense/`\n- Added validation and test coverage for:\n  - idempotent reload,\n  - rollback on mid-run failure,\n  - reference normalization diagnostics.\n\n## Files changed\n\n- `server/app/db/models.py`\n  - Added models for:\n    - `location_master`\n    - `ward_profile`\n    - `lake_reference`\n    - `weather_observation`\n    - `air_quality_observation`\n    - `lake_hydrology`\n    - `power_outage_event`\n    - `traffic_segment_observation`\n    - `flood_incident`\n    - `sessions`\n    - `conversation_history`\n    - `audit_log`\n  - Existing `User` model remains.\n\n- `server/alembic/versions/20260311_0002_metrosense_schema.py`\n  - Creates all MetroSense tables, foreign keys, and core indexes.\n\n- `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`\n  - CSV-driven loader (direct reads via `csv.DictReader`).\n  - Parses source timestamps as IST (`Asia/Kolkata`) into timezone-aware DB values.\n  - Performs replace-style loading (`TRUNCATE` + reload) inside a single transaction.\n  - Normalizes references (`location_master`, `ward_profile`, `lake_reference`) before fact loads.\n  - Adds explicit validation for location/lake coverage before commit.\n  - Supports failure simulation: `--simulate-failure-after-truncate`.\n\n- `server/tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py`\n  - Tests:\n    - replace + idempotent behavior\n    - rollback safety on simulated crash\n    - clear diagnostics for missing normalized location IDs\n\n- `server/scripts/__init__.py`\n  - Added so `scripts` is importable in tests.\n\n## How to run\n\nFrom `server/`:\n\n```bash\nuv run alembic upgrade head\nuv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace\n```\n\n## Loader behavior\n\n- Reads directly from the golden CSV files in `server/DataSet_MetroSense/`.\n- Loads reference tables first, then fact/event tables.\n- Uses one transaction; if anything fails, the whole load is rolled back.\n- `--replace` is required to avoid partial/unclear state semantics.\n\n## Verification commands used\n\nFrom `server/`:\n\n```bash\nuv run ruff check app/db/models.py scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py\nuv run mypy scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py app/db/models.py tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py\nuv run pytest tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py -q\nuv run pytest tests/unit -q\nuv run pytest tests/integration -q\n```\n\n"
  }
}
```

    "output": "Chunk ID: da1660\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 691\nOutput:\n# MetroSense Dataset Schema and Loader\n\nThis documents the backend changes implemented for loading MetroSense golden data into PostgreSQL.\n\n## What was implemented\n\n- Added MetroSense database schema (12 architecture tables) while keeping existing auth `users` table.\n- Added a new Alembic migration to create the schema.\n- Added an idempotent loader that reads directly from CSV files in:\n  - `server/DataSet_MetroSense/`\n- Added validation and test coverage for:\n  - idempotent reload,\n  - rollback on mid-run failure,\n  - reference normalization diagnostics.\n\n## Files changed\n\n- `server/app/db/models.py`\n  - Added models for:\n    - `location_master`\n    - `ward_profile`\n    - `lake_reference`\n    - `weather_observation`\n    - `air_quality_observation`\n    - `lake_hydrology`\n    - `power_outage_event`\n    - `traffic_segment_observation`\n    - `flood_incident`\n    - `sessions`\n    - `conversation_history`\n    - `audit_log`\n  - Existing `User` model remains.\n\n- `server/alembic/versions/20260311_0002_metrosense_schema.py`\n  - Creates all MetroSense tables, foreign keys, and core indexes.\n\n- `server/scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py`\n  - CSV-driven loader (direct reads via `csv.DictReader`).\n  - Parses source timestamps as IST (`Asia/Kolkata`) into timezone-aware DB values.\n  - Performs replace-style loading (`TRUNCATE` + reload) inside a single transaction.\n  - Normalizes references (`location_master`, `ward_profile`, `lake_reference`) before fact loads.\n  - Adds explicit validation for location/lake coverage before commit.\n  - Supports failure simulation: `--simulate-failure-after-truncate`.\n\n- `server/tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py`\n  - Tests:\n    - replace + idempotent behavior\n    - rollback safety on simulated crash\n    - clear diagnostics for missing normalized location IDs\n\n- `server/scripts/__init__.py`\n  - Added so `scripts` is importable in tests.\n\n## How to run\n\nFrom `server/`:\n\n```bash\nuv run alembic upgrade head\nuv run python scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py --replace\n```\n\n## Loader behavior\n\n- Reads directly from the golden CSV files in `server/DataSet_MetroSense/`.\n- Loads reference tables first, then fact/event tables.\n- Uses one transaction; if anything fails, the whole load is rolled back.\n- `--replace` is required to avoid partial/unclear state semantics.\n\n## Verification commands used\n\nFrom `server/`:\n\n```bash\nuv run ruff check app/db/models.py scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py\nuv run mypy scripts/load_metrosense_dataset.py app/db/models.py tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py\nuv run pytest tests/integration/test_metrosense_dataset_loader.py -q\nuv run pytest tests/unit -q\nuv run pytest tests/integration -q\n```\n\n"
  }
}
```
